Home / Alternatives to Freshdesk / Freshdesk vs FreeScout

Freshdesk vs FreeScout

A side-by-side look at Freshdesk (the paid SaaS) and FreeScout (the open source alternative). Use this page to decide if the switch fits your team and workflow.

Freshdesk FreeScout
Tagline Customer support ticketing and omnichannel helpdesk. Lightweight self-hosted Help Scout alternative.
License Proprietary SaaS AGPL-3.0
Pricing Free up to 10 agents; Growth from $15/agent/month; Pro and Enterprise higher. Free to self-host
Self-host option No Yes — difficulty 2/5
Hosted cloud available Yes (only option) No
Desktop apps Varies by product Web only
Mobile apps Official apps typically available iOS, Android
Ad slot — between tables

Best for

Lean teams wanting a Help Scout-style shared inbox on a cheap VPS.

FreeScout strengths

  • Email-first inbox, minimal and fast.
  • Runs on standard LAMP hosting.
  • Good free module selection.

FreeScout weaknesses

  • Fewer channels than Chatwoot (email primarily).
  • Premium modules for advanced features.
  • Smaller community.

What's the catch with Freshdesk?

  • Mailbox sending limits on lower tiers.
  • Key automation and SLA tools gated to Pro+.
  • Many features feel bolted on via the Freshworks suite.

Still unsure?

Check the full list of alternatives to Freshdesk: see Freshdesk alternatives, or learn more about FreeScout on its project page.

Other comparisons

Freshdesk vs Chatwoot

Multi-channel support with live chat, WhatsApp and email in one inbox.

Freshdesk vs Zammad

Traditional helpdesk ticketing with SLAs, shared mailboxes and KB.

Freshdesk vs osTicket

Battle-tested PHP ticketing when you just need a stable issue queue.
